question: What is Notes Helper
ground_truth: A RAG-based tool designed to help you reason across your personal data directory.

question: What can Notes Helper do
ground_truth: Answer questions based on notes in `data/`. Summarize concepts using the most relevant chunks. Help review and recall topics from notes. Show what files were used to generate the answer.

question: What are the constraints of Notes Helper
ground_truth: Notes Helper cannot answer questions outside the stored notes. Answers depend on the quality and completeness of the notes. The system may return short or conservative answers if the notes are short.

question: How does the system retrieve information
ground_truth: User queries are embedded using a Sentence Transformer. The top-k relevant chunks are retrieved. The LLM generates an answer strictly from those chunks.

question: Explain how Notes Helper works
ground_truth: Notes in `data/` are read using memory-safe streaming loaders. Text is split into overlapping chunks. Each chunk is embedded using a Sentence Transformer. Embeddings are indexed with Faiss. User queries are embedded using the same model. Only the top-k relevant chunks are retrieved. LLM generates an answer strictly from those chunks.

question: Can Notes Helper use external knowledge
ground_truth: No, Notes Helper cannot use external knowledge.
